short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $93.67B in FY2026 Q1, +4.4% YoY — stable trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $96.80B (+3.3% vs current Q) — set by 25 broker contributors.
Next earnings: 2026-08-15 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $371B — in cyclical drawdown.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 23.6% — low (premium-buying favored).
Put/Call OI: 0.64 — call-heavy positioning (consensus bullish).

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 18.3% across 7 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 7 dates spanning 98–616 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±8.9% ($441.01 – $527.11).
398-day implied move: ±20.0% ($387.21 – $580.91) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $510 (+5.4% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-5.3pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Bear put spread 6-month: long $460 / short $411. Defined-cost downside positioning; cheaper than outright long puts; captures 10% further downside efficiently.
